title: Profile a hot path with cProfile and pstats
description: Run cProfile on a representative workload, sort by cumulative time, and report the top callers instead of guessing at the bottleneck.
body: Use when a Python path is reported slow and the cause is unknown. Actions: run `python -m cProfile -o out.prof script.py` (or cProfile.Profile() around the hot block), load with pstats and `sort_stats('cumulative').print_stats(20)`, and compare tottime vs cumtime to separate self-time from callee-time; for visual review export to snakeviz. Report the top functions, their call counts, and the suspected cause. Do not micro-optimize before measuring or profile a toy input that misses the real distribution. This skill exposes capability only and must not run production workloads without runtime permission. Validation signals include a profile where the named hot function dominates cumtime and a before/after benchmark after the fix. Risk boundary: if the cost is in a C extension or IO wait, report that cProfile undercounts it and recommend py-spy or tracing instead of forcing a Python-level change.
